title: Untrusted Inputs, Integrity, Privacy, Release, Rollback, and Defense · Failure Triage
summary: A preserved Bookbot run contains one plausible path, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, reporting, CLI, testing, performance, packaging, security, or release defect; identify the first failed boundary before changing code. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in production Bookbot release and residual-risk defense and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causa…
objectives: Threat-model paths, devices, links, huge lines, malformed encodings, control characters, output injection, resource exhaustion, and race limits.
Keep reports and logs to authorized metrics and safe identifiers, with explicit excerpt, retention, deletion, and sharing policy.
Use a modern digest for content identity while distinguishing accidental-corruption evidence from authenticated provenance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Production Defense
steps.instruction:
Order the Production Defense Bookbot and Python corpus-to-release evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Production Defense.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Production Defense assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Bookbot and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Production Defense, keep Production Defense connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.text: A debug Bookbot team handles case 8959 during an assistive-technology terminal review. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable Bookbot release across quality, accessibility, security, capacity, rollback, recovery, and residual risk; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Defend user value, corpus authority, text policy, correctness, determinism, accessibility, security, capacity, packaging, recovery, ownership, and residual risk as one release.

content.text: Order work so Production Defense has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Production Defense
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-production-defense-1wm0y8" that uses case 4437 to defend an immutable Bookbot release across quality, accessibility, security, capacity, rollback, recovery, and residual risk. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Production Defense.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Production Defense into observable performance evidence. For Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Production Defense, keep Production Defense connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-production-defense-1wm0y8
# Competency: bookbot-production-defense.
# Case 4437: a clean-environment package release.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects.
# Changed case: change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy.
def evidence_bookbot_production_defense_1wm0y8(correctness: bool = True, accessibility: bool = True, security: bool = True, performance: bool = True, reproducible_artifact: bool = True, rollback: bool = True, recovery: bool = True, residual_risk_owned: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1wm0y8 = "1wm0y8-8y0mw1-4437"
    assert evidence_variant_1wm0y8
    evidence_axes_1wm0y8 = (
        "corpus1wm0y8",
        "policy8y0mw1",
        "revision4437",
        "command1wm0y84437",
        "report8y0mw14437",
        "test44371wm0y8",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1wm0y8)) == 6
    gates = (correctness, accessibility, security, performance, reproducible_artifact, rollback, recovery)
    passed = sum(1 for gate in gates if gate)
    if not correctness or not accessibility:
        return False, passed, "quality-gates"
    if not security or not performance:
        return False, passed, "risk-gates"
    if not reproducible_artifact or not rollback or not recovery:
        return False, passed, "release-gates"
    if not residual_risk_owned:
        return False, passed, "unowned-risk"
    assert passed == len(gates)
    return True, passed, "production-defense"
